26	RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod \
27	    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=${TARGETOS:-linux} GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build \
28	      -ldflags="-s -w" \
29	      -o /out/claude-subagent \
30	      ./cmd/claude-subagent
31	
32	# Stage 2: Claude Code CLI runtime image.
33	# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 is required because the claude CLI is a Node binary. The CLI
34	# version is pinned (Pitfall 3 — RESEARCH.md: never npm install without an
35	# explicit @<version>); pin tracks the planner's choice on the STACK floor of
36	# v2.1.139. The signed-token credential is injected at K8s Pod-spec time by
37	# the credproxy sidecar (Phase 2 D-C1..C4) — never baked into the image.
38	# Host configuration directories are not bind-mounted (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern).
39	#
40	# git IS required at the executor (task) level: EnsureWorktree shells out to the
41	# git CLI's `git worktree add` (pkg/git/worktree.go — go-git/v5 does not expose
42	# linked worktrees). Clone still uses go-git's pure-Go HTTP transport, but the
43	# per-Task worktree the executor authors into needs the real git binary, so
44	# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 (Debian) gets git installed. Prior to Phase 10 no executor ever
45	# ran end-to-end, so this gap stayed latent.

# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
# Build the claude-subagent runtime image.
# Invoked from REPO ROOT:
#   docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:test \
#                -f images/claude-subagent/Dockerfile .
#
# Stage 1: compile a static Linux Go binary.
FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:1.26-alpine@sha256:a6a091eac01ceac4b97496fe2957a49b6cdd83365337d5f46f6f73710424e805 AS builder
ARG TARGETOS
ARG TARGETARCH

WORKDIR /workspace

# Cache the dependency layer before copying source.
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod go mod download

# Copy only what the binary needs (see .dockerignore for exclusions).
COPY pkg/dispatch/ pkg/dispatch/
COPY internal/harness/ internal/harness/
COPY internal/subagent/ internal/subagent/
COPY pkg/git/ pkg/git/
COPY cmd/claude-subagent/ cmd/claude-subagent/

# Static binary: no CGO, stripped symbols for a smaller image.
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod \
    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=${TARGETOS:-linux} GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build \
      -ldflags="-s -w" \
      -o /out/claude-subagent \
      ./cmd/claude-subagent

# Stage 2: Claude Code CLI runtime image.
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 is required because the claude CLI is a Node binary. The CLI
# version is pinned (Pitfall 3 — RESEARCH.md: never npm install without an
# explicit @<version>); pin tracks the planner's choice on the STACK floor of
# v2.1.139. The signed-token credential is injected at K8s Pod-spec time by
# the credproxy sidecar (Phase 2 D-C1..C4) — never baked into the image.
# Host configuration directories are not bind-mounted (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern).
#
# git IS required at the executor (task) level: EnsureWorktree shells out to the
# git CLI's `git worktree add` (pkg/git/worktree.go — go-git/v5 does not expose
# linked worktrees). Clone still uses go-git's pure-Go HTTP transport, but the
# per-Task worktree the executor authors into needs the real git binary, so
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 (Debian) gets git installed. Prior to Phase 10 no executor ever
# ran end-to-end, so this gap stayed latent.
FROM node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732

RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    # The per-Project repo (/workspace/repo.git) is cloned by the tide-push Job
    # (uid 65532) but the executor runs as uid 1000 — git 2.35+ refuses a repo
    # it does not own ("detected dubious ownership"). The pod is the trust
    # boundary (hermetic, single-purpose), so mark all repos safe at the system
    # level. This covers our `git worktree add` shell-out AND any git the claude
    # CLI itself runs inside the worktree (whose .git points back to repo.git).
    && git config --system --add safe.directory '*'

RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.142

COPY --from=builder /out/claude-subagent /usr/local/bin/claude-subagent

# Run as non-root UID 1000 — matches D-G3 subagent harness UID requirement.
USER 1000

ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/claude-subagent"]

17	package v1alpha1
18	
19	import (
20		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
21	)
22	
23	// SecretRefs declares the K8s Secret names that carry credentials.
24	// Per AUTH-01, populated in Phase 3; Phase 1 ships the field shape only.
25	type SecretRefs struct {
26		// AnthropicAPIKey is the Secret name carrying the LLM API key.
27		// +optional
28		AnthropicAPIKey string `json:"anthropicAPIKey,omitempty"`
29		// GitCredentials is the Secret name carrying git push credentials (PAT or SSH key).
30		// +optional
31		GitCredentials string `json:"gitCredentials,omitempty"`
32	}
33	
34	// ModelSelection picks per-level model identifiers.
35	// Phase 1 ships the field shape; Phase 2+ consumes.
36	type ModelSelection struct {
37		// +optional
38		Milestone string `json:"milestone,omitempty"`
39		// +optional
40		Phase string `json:"phase,omitempty"`
41		// +optional
42		Plan string `json:"plan,omitempty"`
43		// +optional
44		Task string `json:"task,omitempty"`
45	}
46	
47	// GatePolicy is one of "auto" | "approve" | "pause" — per-level human gate.
48	// Phase 1 ships the field shape; Phase 4 consumes.
49	// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=auto;approve;pause
50	type GatePolicy string
51	
52	// Gates declares per-level gate policy. Phase 1 ships field; Phase 4 wires.
53	type Gates struct {
54		// +optional
55		Milestone GatePolicy `json:"milestone,omitempty"`
56		// +optional
57		Phase GatePolicy `json:"phase,omitempty"`
58		// +optional
59		Plan GatePolicy `json:"plan,omitempty"`
60		// +optional
61		Task GatePolicy `json:"task,omitempty"`
62		// +optional
63		PauseBetweenWaves bool `json:"pauseBetweenWaves,omitempty"`
64	}
65	
66	// RouteSpec is a (method, path-prefix) tuple used to extend the credproxy
67	// upstream allowlist for a Project (Phase 04.1 P4.2). The credproxy sidecar
68	// always enforces the hardcoded baseline (POST /v1/messages and
69	// POST /v1/messages/count_tokens); RouteSpec entries are additive extensions,
70	// never replacements.
71	type RouteSpec struct {
72		// Method is the HTTP method. Currently only GET and POST are accepted.
73		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=GET;POST
74		Method string `json:"method"`
75	
76		// PathPrefix is the URL path prefix. Must start with "/v1/" — paths
77		// outside the Anthropic API surface are rejected at admission.
78		// +kubebuilder:validation:Pattern=`^/v1/[a-zA-Z0-9_/-]+$`
79		PathPrefix string `json:"pathPrefix"`
80	}
81	
82	// ProviderConfig configures one LLM provider (Phase 2+).
83	type ProviderConfig struct {
84		// Name is the provider identifier (only "anthropic" is supported in v1).
85		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=anthropic
86		Name string `json:"name"`
87	
88		// RequestsPerMinute optionally caps API requests per minute for this provider.
89		// +optional
90		RequestsPerMinute *int32 `json:"requestsPerMinute,omitempty"`
91	
92		// TokensPerMinute optionally caps token throughput per minute for this provider.
93		// +optional
94		TokensPerMinute *int32 `json:"tokensPerMinute,omitempty"`
95	
96		// AllowedRoutes optionally extends the hardcoded credproxy allowlist
97		// (POST /v1/messages + POST /v1/messages/count_tokens) with additional
98		// (method, path-prefix) tuples. Use this to grant the subagent access
99		// to newly-released LLM endpoints (Files API, Search Tools, prompt
100		// caching) without rebuilding the credproxy image.
101		// Hardcoded safe defaults ALWAYS apply — operator additions are
102		// additive, never restrictive. Admin/billing paths are rejected at
103		// admission (defense in depth).
104		// +optional
105		AllowedRoutes []RouteSpec `json:"allowedRoutes,omitempty"`
106	}
107	
108	// BudgetConfig declares cost/token caps for the Project (Phase 2+).
109	type BudgetConfig struct {
110		// AbsoluteCapCents is the hard spending limit in USD cents for the project lifetime.
111		// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=0
112		AbsoluteCapCents int64 `json:"absoluteCapCents"`
113	
114		// RollingWindowCapCents caps spending over the rolling window defined by
115		// BudgetStatus.WindowStart and BudgetConfig.RollingWindowDuration. When
116		// the window elapses, ProjectReconciler.handleBudgetGate resets
117		// CostSpentCents + TokensSpent and advances WindowStart.
118		// +optional
119		RollingWindowCapCents int64 `json:"rollingWindowCapCents,omitempty"`
120	
121		// RollingWindowDuration is the window length over which
122		// RollingWindowCapCents applies. Default: 24h when unset. Must be >= 1h
123		// to prevent inadvertent denial-of-dispatch via tiny windows.
124		// Validation: enforced semantically in ProjectReconciler (metav1.Duration
125		// is a struct; controller-gen Pattern markers require a string type).
126		// +optional
127		RollingWindowDuration *metav1.Duration `json:"rollingWindowDuration,omitempty"`
128	}
129	
130	// PlanAdmissionConfig controls file-touch policy during plan validation (Phase 2+).
131	type PlanAdmissionConfig struct {
132		// FileTouchMode determines how file-touch mismatches are handled:
133		//   "strict" — reject plans whose file touches deviate from declarations.
134		//   "warn"   — admit but annotate the mismatch on Plan.Status.
135		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=strict;warn
136		// +optional
137		FileTouchMode string `json:"fileTouchMode,omitempty"`
138	}
139	
140	// SubagentConfig declares per-Project provider+model selection (Phase 3 D-C2).
141	//
142	// Resolution chain at dispatch time (in each reconciler before calling
143	// Dispatcher.Run): Spec.Subagent.Levels.{level}.{image,model} → Spec.Subagent.{image,model}
144	// → Helm-chart default. Vendor + model are orthogonal axes — Image picks the
145	// vendor (via the container image's bundled Subagent impl); Model picks the
146	// model identifier passed to that vendor's CLI/SDK.
147	type SubagentConfig struct {
148		// Image is the default subagent image reference for all levels.
149		// Example: "ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:v0.1.0".
150		// +optional
151		Image string `json:"image,omitempty"`
152	
153		// Model is the default model identifier for all levels.
154		// Example: "claude-sonnet-4-6" (anthropic), "o1-mini" (openai).
155		// +optional
156		Model string `json:"model,omitempty"`
157	
158		// Levels carries per-level overrides; any subset is valid.
159		// +optional
160		Levels LevelOverrides `json:"levels,omitempty"`
161	}
162	
163	// LevelOverrides carries per-level provider/model overrides (Phase 3 D-C2).
164	// Each pointer is nil when no override is set for that level.
165	type LevelOverrides struct {
166		// Milestone optionally overrides settings for the milestone planner.
167		// +optional
168		Milestone *LevelConfig `json:"milestone,omitempty"`
169	
170		// Phase optionally overrides settings for the phase planner.
171		// +optional
172		Phase *LevelConfig `json:"phase,omitempty"`
173	
174		// Plan optionally overrides settings for the plan planner.
175		// +optional
176		Plan *LevelConfig `json:"plan,omitempty"`
177	
178		// Task optionally overrides settings for the task executor.
179		// +optional
180		Task *LevelConfig `json:"task,omitempty"`
181	}
182	
183	// LevelConfig declares the per-level subagent override (Phase 3 D-C2).
184	type LevelConfig struct {
185		// Model identifier for this level (e.g., "claude-haiku-4-5").
186		// +optional
187		Model string `json:"model,omitempty"`
188	
189		// Params carries per-vendor tuning passthrough (temperature, thinking
190		// budget, etc.). Validation lives in the provider's Subagent impl.
191		// +optional
192		Params map[string]string `json:"params,omitempty"`
193	
194		// Image override is schema-present-but-not-enforced in v1.0 (deferred
195		// to v1.x per CONTEXT.md "Deferred Ideas" — per-level cross-vendor
196		// image overrides). Set this in v1.0 only if the consumer is prepared
197		// to ignore it.
198		// +optional
199		Image string `json:"image,omitempty"`
200	}
201	
202	// GitConfig declares the per-Project git target + creds (Phase 3 D-B6).
203	// HTTPS+PAT is the primary v1.0 path; SSH is documented with host-key caveats
204	// per REQUIREMENTS.md ART-05 but defaults to HTTPS+PAT.
205	type GitConfig struct {
206		// RepoURL is the URL of the target git remote. Supports http:// and https://
207		// (pure-Go go-git transport, production default) and SSH
208		// (git@host:owner/repo.git, documented with host-key caveats).
209		// file:// is NOT supported: go-git's file:// transport shells out to a
210		// system git binary absent from production images.
211		// +kubebuilder:validation:Pattern=`^(https?://|git@).+`
212		RepoURL string `json:"repoURL"`
213	
214		// CredsSecretRef is the K8s Secret name (same-namespace) carrying the
215		// HTTPS PAT in data key GIT_PAT. Cross-namespace Secret refs are NOT
216		// permitted in v1.0 (threat-mitigation deferred to push Job — plan 03-06).
217		CredsSecretRef string `json:"credsSecretRef"`
218	
219		// LeaksConfigRef is an optional ConfigMap name carrying gitleaks rule
220		// overrides (D-B3). Default gitleaks rules are embedded in the push image
221		// when this is empty.
222		// +optional
223		LeaksConfigRef string `json:"leaksConfigRef,omitempty"`
224	}
225	
226	// GitStatus records the per-Project git push state (Phase 3 D-B6).
227	//
228	// One branch per Project lifetime: BranchName is fixed at Project creation
229	// as "tide/run-<project-name>-<unix-epoch>" (Unix epoch keeps refnames
230	// colon-free per RFC 3987 refname rules). LastPushedSHA + LeaseFailureCount
231	// drive the --force-with-lease push contract: each push uses lastPushedSHA
232	// as the lease, increments leaseFailureCount on rejection, and resets to 0
233	// on success.
234	type GitStatus struct {
235		// BranchName is the lifetime branch fixed at Project creation.
236		// Format: "tide/run-<project>-<unix-epoch>".
237		// +optional
238		BranchName string `json:"branchName,omitempty"`
239	
240		// LastPushedSHA is the head SHA recorded on the most recent successful push.
241		// Used as the lease for subsequent `git push --force-with-lease`.
242		// +optional
243		LastPushedSHA string `json:"lastPushedSHA,omitempty"`
244	
245		// LeaseFailureCount tallies consecutive push lease rejections; reset to 0
246		// on successful push. Reconciler halts (Phase=PushLeaseFailed) when this
247		// count exceeds the configured retry budget.
248		// +optional
249		LeaseFailureCount int32 `json:"leaseFailureCount,omitempty"`
250	}
251	
252	// BudgetStatus records running spend tallies for the Project (Phase 2+).
253	// PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: this is a TALLY object, not an aggregate schedule.
254	// The PERSIST-02 denylist (enforced by `make verify-no-aggregates`) does not
255	// apply to this struct — it carries only spend counters (tokensSpent,
256	// costSpentCents, windowStart), not a derived execution plan.
257	type BudgetStatus struct {
258		// TokensSpent is the cumulative token count spent since WindowStart.
259		// +optional
260		TokensSpent int64 `json:"tokensSpent,omitempty"`
261	
262		// CostSpentCents is the cumulative cost in USD cents since WindowStart.
263		// +optional
264		CostSpentCents int64 `json:"costSpentCents,omitempty"`
265	
266		// WindowStart marks the beginning of the current rolling budget window.
267		// +optional
268		WindowStart *metav1.Time `json:"windowStart,omitempty"`
269	}
270	
271	// BoundaryPushStatus records the bounded auto-retry state of the project-level
272	// boundary push (debug defect #13b). The boundary push lands the already-
273	// integrated run branch on the remote AFTER the Project reaches Complete; it is
274	// observability + bounded recovery, NEVER a gate on Complete.
275	//
276	// PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: this is a per-project retry tally object, NOT an
277	// aggregate schedule. Attempts is re-derived from .status on every reconcile so
278	// the bounded retry survives a controller restart (no in-memory counter).
279	type BoundaryPushStatus struct {
280		// Attempts is the number of boundary-push Jobs dispatched so far for this
281		// Project's run branch. The controller stops dispatching once Attempts
282		// reaches the in-controller cap (maxBoundaryPushAttempts) — bounded retry,
283		// no push-loop.
284		// +optional
285		Attempts int32 `json:"attempts,omitempty"`
286	
287		// LastAttemptTime is the timestamp of the most recently dispatched boundary
288		// push attempt. Drives the capped exponential requeue backoff.
289		// +optional
290		LastAttemptTime *metav1.Time `json:"lastAttemptTime,omitempty"`
291	
292		// LastError carries the most recent boundary-push failure reason (from the
293		// push-result envelope) for operator visibility. Cleared on success.
294		// +optional
295		LastError string `json:"lastError,omitempty"`
296	}
297	
298	// ProjectSpec defines the desired state of Project.
299	// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.targetRepo.startsWith('http://') || self.targetRepo.startsWith('https://') || self.targetRepo.startsWith('git@')",message="targetRepo must be an http(s) or SSH (git@) URL; file:// is not a supported production transport (go-git's file:// transport requires a system git binary absent from production images)"
300	type ProjectSpec struct {
301		// TargetRepo is the URL of the repo this Project operates on. Supports
302		// http:// and https:// (pure-Go go-git transport, production default) and
303		// SSH (`git@host:owner/repo.git`, documented with host-key caveats).
304		// file:// is NOT supported: go-git's file:// transport requires a system
305		// git binary absent from production images.
306		// +kubebuilder:validation:MinLength=1
307		TargetRepo string `json:"targetRepo"`
308	
309		// OutcomePrompt is the human-authored outcome statement that TIDE turns
310		// into a phase brief + PLAN.md + tasks (Phase 5 BOOT-04). The planner
311		// subagent reads this verbatim from .spec; the v1.0 stub-subagent ignores
312		// it (small samples can leave it empty for the smoke-test path).
313		// Multi-line YAML literals (`|`) are the conventional shape.
314		// +optional
315		OutcomePrompt string `json:"outcomePrompt,omitempty"`
316	
317		// SecretRefs references K8s Secrets for credentials (AUTH-01 — Phase 3).
318		// +optional
319		SecretRefs SecretRefs `json:"secretRefs,omitempty"`
320	
321		// ModelSelection picks per-level model identifiers (Phase 2+).
322		// +optional
323		ModelSelection ModelSelection `json:"modelSelection,omitempty"`
324	
325		// Gates declares per-level human gate policy (Phase 4).
326		// +optional
327		Gates Gates `json:"gates,omitempty"`
328	
329		// ProviderSecretRef is the name of the K8s Secret carrying provider credentials (Phase 2+).
330		// +optional
331		ProviderSecretRef string `json:"providerSecretRef,omitempty"`
332	
333		// Providers lists per-provider configuration (rate limits, etc.) (Phase 2+).
334		// +optional
335		Providers []ProviderConfig `json:"providers,omitempty"`
336	
337		// Budget declares cost/token caps for this project (Phase 2+).
338		// +optional
339		Budget BudgetConfig `json:"budget,omitempty"`
340	
341		// PlanAdmission controls file-touch policy during plan validation (Phase 2+).
342		// +optional
343		PlanAdmission PlanAdmissionConfig `json:"planAdmission,omitempty"`
344	
345		// MaxAttemptsPerTask caps the number of dispatch attempts per Task before
346		// the Task is marked failed (Phase 2+, consumed by TaskReconciler in Plan 09).
347		// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1
348		// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum=10
349		// +optional
350		MaxAttemptsPerTask int32 `json:"maxAttemptsPerTask,omitempty"`
351	
352		// Subagent declares provider+model selection for planner/executor dispatch
353		// (Phase 3 D-C2). When empty, Helm-chart defaults apply.
354		// +optional
355		Subagent SubagentConfig `json:"subagent,omitempty"`
356	
357		// Git declares the per-Project target repo + creds for artifact push
358		// (Phase 3 D-B6). Required for any Project whose lifecycle reaches push;
359		// optional in v1.0 only for purely transient/test Projects.
360		// Pointer so omitempty fully elides the field when absent — value-type
361		// GitConfig{} would serialize as `git: {repoURL: ""}` and trip the
362		// RepoURL pattern validation on existing Phase 2 test fixtures.
363		// +optional
364		Git *GitConfig `json:"git,omitempty"`
365	}
366	
367	// Project Phase constants for Project.Status.Phase (Plan 10 — init Job + budget gate).
368	const (
369		// PhasePending is the initial phase before any reconcile has run.
370		PhasePending = "Pending"
371		// PhaseInitialized is set when the init Job completes successfully.
372		PhaseInitialized = "Initialized"
373		// PhaseInitFailed is set when the init Job exits non-zero.
374		PhaseInitFailed = "InitFailed"
375		// PhaseBudgetExceeded is set when the project's absolute cost cap is exceeded.
376		PhaseBudgetExceeded = "BudgetExceeded"
377		// PhaseRunning is set when dispatch is actively proceeding.
378		PhaseRunning = "Running"
379		// PhasePushLeaseFailed is set when a push Job rejects due to --force-with-lease
380		// mismatch (Phase 3 D-B6). Recovery: kubectl annotate project
381		// tideproject.k8s/bypass-push-lease=true (mirrors D-D4 bypass-budget pattern).
382		PhasePushLeaseFailed = "PushLeaseFailed"
383		// PhasePushLeakBlocked is set when a push Job exits with code 10 (gitleaks
384		// finding — envelope.reason=leak-detected). Distinct from PhasePushLeaseFailed
385		// (exit-11) so the project_controller switch can fire
386		// tide_secret_leak_blocked_total only on the leak-class outcome (Phase 4 D-W1).
387		// Recovery: operator inspects the diff and either drops the leaked secret
388		// artifact or rotates the leaked credential, then clears the phase.
389		PhasePushLeakBlocked = "PushLeakBlocked"
390		// PhaseComplete is the terminal success phase — set when all Milestones
391		// reach Succeeded and the final push Job lands (Phase 3 D-B2 #4).
392		PhaseComplete = "Complete"
393	)
394	
395	// ProjectStatus defines the observed state of Project.
396	// PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: NO aggregate schedule fields here.
397	type ProjectStatus struct {
398		// Phase is a high-level state label ("Pending", "Running", "Complete", "Failed").
399		// +optional
400		Phase string `json:"phase,omitempty"`
401	
402		// Conditions follow the standard K8s convention.
403		// +listType=map
404		// +listMapKey=type
405		// +optional
406		Conditions []metav1.Condition `json:"conditions,omitempty"`
407	
408		// Budget records running spend tallies (Phase 2+).
409		// PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: BudgetStatus is a tally object, not an aggregate schedule.
410		// +optional
411		Budget BudgetStatus `json:"budget,omitempty"`
412	
413		// Git records per-Project push state — branch name, last pushed SHA, and
414		// lease failure counter (Phase 3 D-B6). PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: this is a
415		// per-project tally object, NOT an aggregate schedule.
416		// +optional
417		Git GitStatus `json:"git,omitempty"`
418	
419		// BoundaryPush records the bounded auto-retry state of the project-level
420		// boundary push (debug defect #13b). NEVER gates Complete — surfaced via the
421		// non-terminal BoundaryPushed condition for observability + bounded recovery.
422		// +optional
423		BoundaryPush BoundaryPushStatus `json:"boundaryPush,omitempty"`
424	}

435		metav1.TypeMeta `json:",inline"`
436	
437		// metadata is a standard object metadata
438		// +optional
439		metav1.ObjectMeta `json:"metadata,omitzero"`

250	func MaterializeChildCRDs(ctx context.Context, c client.Client, scheme *runtime.Scheme, parent metav1.Object, children []pkgdispatch.ChildCRDSpec) error {
251		return reporter.MaterializeChildCRDs(ctx, c, scheme, parent, children)
252	}
253	
254	// resolveImage walks Project.Spec.Subagent precedence chain for the given
255	// level, returning the resolved subagent container image reference.
256	//
257	//	Levels.<level>.Image → Spec.Subagent.Image → helmDefaults.Image → ""
258	//
259	// An empty return means no image was configured; callers must surface this
260	// as a config error rather than dispatching a Job with an empty image field.
261	// Level "project" has no entry in the switch (the CRD has no Levels.Project);
262	// resolution falls straight to Spec.Subagent.Image, then helmDefaults.Image.
263	func resolveImage(project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project, level string, helmDefaults ProviderDefaults) string {
264		var levelCfg *tideprojectv1alpha1.LevelConfig
265		if project != nil {
266			switch level {
267			case "milestone":
268				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Milestone
269			case "phase":
270				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Phase
271			case "plan":
272				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Plan
273			case "task":
274				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Task
275			}
276		}
277		switch {
278		case levelCfg != nil && levelCfg.Image != "":
279			return levelCfg.Image
280		case project != nil && project.Spec.Subagent.Image != "":
281			return project.Spec.Subagent.Image
282		default:
283			return helmDefaults.Image
284		}
285	}
286	
287	// checkParentApproval implements the D-02 descent hold — children materialize
288	// but dispatch waits for parental approval (tidal lock pending).
289	//
290	// Returns (true, nil) when the direct parent is parked at AwaitingApproval,
291	// signalling that the child reconciler must hold Job dispatch with a 5s requeue.
292	// Returns (false, nil) when parentName is empty (root level — no parent to check)
293	// or when the parent is not found (client.IgnoreNotFound — NotFound is transient
294	// informer lag; callers continue dispatch and the next reconcile re-checks).
295	// Non-NotFound Get errors propagate to the standard requeue-on-error path.
296	//
297	// The parentKind switch covers "Milestone", "Phase", and "Plan" — the three
298	// parent kinds that can park at AwaitingApproval. Unknown kinds return (false, nil)
299	// (permissive default: unknown parent kind should not block dispatch).
300	//
301	// Design note: held children stay at Status.Phase="" — this helper writes NO
302	// status so tide approve's findAwaiting* cannot target a held child instead of
303	// the parked parent (Pitfall 5 from 12-RESEARCH.md).
304	func checkParentApproval(ctx context.Context, c client.Client, ns, parentName, parentKind string) (bool, error) {
305		if parentName == "" {
306			return false, nil
307		}
308		switch parentKind {
309		case "Milestone":
310			var ms tideprojectv1alpha1.Milestone
311			if err := c.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Namespace: ns, Name: parentName}, &ms); err != nil {
312				return false, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
313			}
314			return ms.Status.Phase == "AwaitingApproval", nil
315		case "Phase":
316			var ph tideprojectv1alpha1.Phase
317			if err := c.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Namespace: ns, Name: parentName}, &ph); err != nil {
318				return false, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
319			}
320			return ph.Status.Phase == "AwaitingApproval", nil
321		case "Plan":
322			var plan tideprojectv1alpha1.Plan
323			if err := c.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Namespace: ns, Name: parentName}, &plan); err != nil {
324				return false, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
325			}
326			return plan.Status.Phase == "AwaitingApproval", nil
327		}
328		return false, nil
329	}

# TIDE controller chart values (Phase 1 surface — D-E1).
#
# This file is the canonical, hand-maintained values.yaml for charts/tide/. The
# `make helm-controller` target generates charts/tide/ via helmify, then copies
# THIS file over the helmify-emitted charts/tide/values.yaml. Helmify cannot
# infer (a) ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* image registry, (b) the Phase 1 tunables
# (plannerConcurrency, executorConcurrency, maxConcurrentReconciles), or (c)
# leaderElection block — those are project-level decisions that live here,
# version-controlled separately from helmify's regenerated output.
#
# Phase 5 will extend this with dashboard.enabled, prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled,
# and additional OSS-distribution flags (D-E2). The structure below — helmify-emitted
# controllerManager.* keys plus hand-augmented top-level Phase 1 tunables — is the
# Phase 5-compatible foundation.

# Image refs (D-A2: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* registry).
controllerManager:
  manager:
    args:
    - --metrics-bind-address=:8443
    - --config=/etc/tide/config.yaml
    - --watch-namespace=$(WATCH_NAMESPACE)
    - --leader-elect
    - --webhook-cert-path=/tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs
    containerSecurityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - ALL
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
    env:
      # watchNamespace = "" means watch ALL namespaces (cluster-scoped install posture,
      # AUTH-02). Set this to a specific namespace for per-namespace install — the
      # cmd/manager/main.go --watch-namespace flag wires it through to the Manager's
      # cache.ByObject namespace filter.
      watchNamespace: ""
    image:
      repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-controller
      tag: ""
      pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    podAnnotations: {}
    resources:
      limits:
        cpu: 500m
        memory: 128Mi
      requests:
        cpu: 10m
        memory: 64Mi
  podSecurityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    seccompProfile:
      type: RuntimeDefault
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccount:
    annotations: {}
kubernetesClusterDomain: cluster.local
metricsService:
  ports:
  - name: https
    port: 8443
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 8443
  type: ClusterIP
webhookService:
  ports:
  - port: 443
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 9443
  type: ClusterIP

# Phase 1 runtime tunables (D-E1) — rendered into the tide-config ConfigMap which is
# mounted at /etc/tide/config.yaml and parsed by internal/config.Load (CTRL-04 / Plan 04).
#
# Two distinct parallelism budgets per Pitfall 6: planning fans out wide (Milestone/
# Phase/Plan reconciles), execution fans out narrow (Wave/Task reconciles). The
# crosspool analyzer (cmd/tide-lint) prevents the two budgets from being unified at
# compile time; this values surface keeps them separately tunable at deploy time.
plannerConcurrency: 16
executorConcurrency: 4

content.content:
# Default rate-limit parameters for provider credential Secrets (D-D3).
# Applied when no Secret annotation or Project.Spec.Providers entry overrides them.
# Passed to the controller via --rate-limit-default-rpm and --rate-limit-default-burst flags.
rateLimits:
  defaults:
    requestsPerMinute: 60
    tokensPerMinute: 100000  # informational v1 — not yet enforced at the bucket level
    burst: 10

# Pricing overrides (D-02): per-model price corrections merged OVER the compiled
# priceTable at controller startup. Operators use this to correct price drift without
# a TIDE release. Keys are exact model ID strings; values are cents per million tokens.
# Passed to the controller via --pricing-overrides-json flag (JSON-encoded map).
#
# Example (all fields optional — omitted fields inherit compiled defaults):
#   pricing:
#     overrides:
#       claude-some-future-model:
#         inputCentsPerMTok: 300
#         outputCentsPerMTok: 1500
#         cacheReadCentsPerMTok: 30
#         cacheWriteCentsPerMTok: 375
pricing:
  overrides: {}

# Phase 2 image refs for credproxy sidecar + build/load tooling (D-A2).
# images.stubSubagent.* keys are retained for image build/load tooling
# (make test-int-kind-prep loads the stub image by tag), but are NO LONGER
# injected as a --subagent-image flag into the Deployment args (Phase 13 D-01;
# that flag was silently forcing the stub in every v1.0.0 install and has been
# dropped). Subagent image default now flows via subagent.defaults.image →
# CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var on the manager Deployment.
#
# Phase 3 plan 03-09 additions: claudeSubagent (D-C2 real-Claude image, swaps
# in alongside stubSubagent; both images stay buildable so chaos-resume and
# kind smoke tests keep working with the stub) and tidePush (D-B1 dedicated
# push-Job image — orchestrator-side gitleaks + go-git push-with-lease).
#
# Production reproducibility: tags are mutable in the OCI spec; pin by
# `@sha256:<digest>` in production overrides (`--set images.tidePush.tag=...`
# with the digest, or set repository: ghcr.io/.../tide-push@sha256:...).
images:
  stubSubagent:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  credProxy:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-credproxy
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  busybox:
    repository: busybox
    tag: "1.36"
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-B1): dedicated tide-push image. Orchestrator-side
  # push Job per level boundary; gitleaks + go-git + --force-with-lease.
  # Wired to controller via TIDE_PUSH_IMAGE env var on manager Deployment.
  tidePush:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-push
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-C2 swap-in): real Claude-Code-backed subagent image.
  # Used as the default fallback when Project.Spec.subagent.image is empty;
  # individual Projects can pin a different vendor image per Project (D-C2).
  # Wired to controller via CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var on manager Deployment.
  # In production, prefer pinning by @sha256 digest — tag-based pulls of mutable tags
  # are not reproducible across pulls.
  claudeSubagent:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

# Per-level models flow through the manager Deployment env block as
# TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_{MILESTONE,PHASE,PLAN,TASK}.
subagent:
  defaults:
    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent
    model: claude-sonnet-4-6
  levels:
    milestone:
      model: claude-opus-4-7
    phase:
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    plan:
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    task:
      model: claude-haiku-4-5

# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-B3): optional cluster-wide gitleaks override.
# Empty (default) → push Job uses the compiled-in gitleaks v8 ruleset.
# Per-Project overrides are still supported via Project.Spec.git.leaksConfigRef
# (D-B3 — ConfigMap mounted into push Job at /etc/tide/gitleaks-config.toml).
# This top-level key is the cluster-wide fallback (v1.0 deferred; reserved for v1.x).
gitleaks:
  configMapName: ""

# HMAC signing-secret name (D-C3 / Blocker #1 fix).
# The signing-secret.yaml Helm template creates a Secret with this name on first install
# using Helm's `lookup` + `if not` guard (idempotent). resource-policy: keep annotation
# preserves the Secret across helm upgrade/uninstall so in-flight tokens stay valid
# (T-02-12-02 / T-02-12-03 mitigations).
# The Secret's data key is TIDE_SIGNING_KEY (env-friendly) so envFrom: [{secretRef: ...}]
# auto-populates the TIDE_SIGNING_KEY env var on the Manager Deployment and credproxy sidecar.
signingKey:
  secretName: tide-signing-key

# Budget defaults (D-D2 / FAIL-04). Helm-driven hard caps applied to all Projects that
# do not specify per-Project caps in Project.Spec.Budget.
budget:
  # D-05: flat per-dispatch reservation estimate in cents pre-charged against the budget
  # cap at Job creation; bounds in-flight overshoot to estimate error instead of wave width;
  # 0 disables pre-charge (cap still enforced at dispatch via the BudgetBlocked condition).
  # Passed via --budget-reserve-per-dispatch-cents.
  reservePerDispatchCents: 100
  defaults:
    absoluteCapCents: 10000      # $100.00 default absolute cap
    rollingWindowCapCents: 5000  # $50.00 / 15-min rolling window default

# Phase 4 additions (Plan 04-14 — D-X3 / D-D2 / D-O6 / D-O3).
#
# All three blocks are ADDITIVE. The Phase 1 surface above is unchanged
# (CLAUDE.md "chart is FIXED contract — binary catches up to chart, never
# reverse" anti-pattern). Phase 4 chart additions land here without touching
# any pre-Phase-4 keys.

# Read-only dashboard backend (Phase 4 D-D2). Ships as a separate
# Deployment + Service + ServiceAccount + ClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding
# under templates/dashboard-{deployment,service,rbac}.yaml. The dashboard
# ClusterRole is enforced read-only at chart-render time via
# `make helm-rbac-assert` (T-04-D2 mitigation).
#
# Default ON because the dashboard is one of the four Phase 4 deliverables;
# operators who want the controller-only install set
# `--set dashboard.enabled=false`.
dashboard:
  enabled: true
  image:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-dashboard
    tag: ""             # default to .Chart.AppVersion
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  replicas: 1
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 50m
      memory: 64Mi
    limits:
      cpu: 200m
      memory: 256Mi
  service:
    type: ClusterIP
    port: 80
    targetPort: 8080

# Prometheus ServiceMonitor (Phase 4 D-O6).
#
# DEFAULT OFF per CLAUDE.md anti-pattern:
#   "Default the chart's ServiceMonitor to prometheus.enabled=false to avoid
#    CRD-not-found on plain clusters."
#
# Enable explicitly via `--set prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled=true` once
# the prometheus-operator CRDs (monitoring.coreos.com/v1) are installed.
prometheus:
  # PromQL-proxy endpoint (Phase 4 telemetry surface -- milestone-01 Q1 lock-in).
  #
  # Q1 DECISION: PROXY, not a second browser-side datasource. This is a
  # SERVER-SIDE value. When non-empty it is injected as the PROM_ENDPOINT env
  # var into the dashboard container (templates/dashboard-deployment.yaml, landed
  # by plan-02), and the dashboard chi server proxies PromQL via
  # GET /api/v1/query and GET /api/v1/query_range. The browser therefore never
  # learns a second network target: single origin, no CORS, one auth surface,
  # and the Prometheus URL stays confined to the dashboard process (matters when
  # Prometheus is ClusterIP-only and unreachable from outside the cluster).

1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Working Rules
6	
7	Project-agnostic operating principles. Apply these before anything else, including before reading the rest of this file.
8	
9	### 1. Observe First
10	
11	Every wasted iteration in Phase 02.2 traced back to acting on assumptions instead of observations. The cascade-5 debug session hypothesized a "Wave Ready check" that didn't exist; the cascade-8 debug session hypothesized "ResourceVersion-conflict exponential-backoff suppression" when the actual cause was a never-assigned `Dispatcher` field. Both were avoidable.
12	
13	1. When a runtime gate is BLOCKED → **read the manager log (`kubectl logs` or `/tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log`) and the VERIFICATION.md frontmatter BEFORE hypothesizing**
14	2. When a controller misbehaves → **`kubectl logs` + `kubectl describe <crd>` + `kubectl get <crd> -o yaml` BEFORE reading reconciler source**
15	3. When a debug hypothesis cites a specific code path → **grep the codebase to confirm it exists in the cited form** (`grep -nE "Wave.*Ready|Ready.*Wave" internal/controller/` would have refuted cascade-5's framing in seconds)
16	4. When a code change has no visible effect → **verify the binary rebuilt (image digest changed) and the Pod rotated (`kubectl get pods -n tide-system` shows new age)**, don't keep tweaking
17	5. When suggesting a Makefile target → **`grep -nE '^target-name:' Makefile`** to confirm it exists before recommending the command
18	6. When an agent could answer a question → **but `kubectl get`, `git log --grep`, or a single grep answers it faster, use the CLI**
19	
20	Subagents are for understanding architecture and authoring artifacts. Runtime artifacts (manager logs, CRD status, git history) are for debugging runtime failures. Do not confuse the two.
21	
22	### 2. Execute, Don't Ask
23	
24	When the next step is obvious and non-destructive AND inside an active GSD workflow: do it. Don't present the command and ask for confirmation. This includes running `make test-int`, rerunning failing tests, creating planning artifacts, applying fixes specified in an approved `PLAN.md`, and committing per the executor's task protocol.
25	
26	**Exceptions — always confirm or refuse:**
27	
28	- **Production code edits outside an active GSD plan.** The `GSD Workflow Enforcement` section below is binding. Even if the fix is obvious, route through `/gsd:quick` or `/gsd:debug` so artifacts stay in sync.
29	- **Edits to `charts/tide/values.yaml`** — chart is FIXED contract per Phase 02.2's anti-pattern. Binary catches up to chart, never reverse.
30	- **`git push --force`** to any branch, especially `main`.
31	- **`git reset --hard`** outside a worktree's own HEAD-correction step.
32	- **`kind delete cluster`** outside an explicit clean-rerun sequence (it destroys all test state).
33	- **`rm -rf`** on `.git/`, `.planning/`, `.claude/`, or any directory containing uncommitted user work.
34	- **`gsd-sdk query state-set ...`** that downgrades phase status or rewrites tracked progress fields.
35	
36	### 3. Verify Before Claiming
37	
38	Never say "this should fix it," "the fix is complete," or "cascade-N is closed." Run the verification. Read the gate_decision. Grep for the artifact. Then state what you observed.
39	
40	Concrete verification protocols for Phase 02.2-style runtime gates:
41	
42	- **Plan landed:** `git log --oneline -1 -- <files_modified>` shows the expected commit on `main`
43	- **VERIFICATION.md exists:** `ls .planning/phases/<dir>/<plan>-VERIFICATION.md` returns a file
44	- **Gate APPROVED:** `grep -cE '^gate_decision: APPROVED$' <VERIFICATION>` returns exactly `1`
45	- **Test ran:** `tail -30 /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` shows Ginkgo summary line `Ran X Specs in Ys`
46	- **Dispatch is live (Phase 02.2-10 evidence shape):** `grep -E '"creating job"|"dispatch"' /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` returns ≥ 1 line, AND `kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces` shows Jobs in the test namespaces
47	- **Cascade closed:** the specific manager-log error that defined the cascade (e.g. `"no project found in namespace X"`) returns 0 in the new run's log
48	- **`make test-int` exit ≠ "Ginkgo green."** The `test/integration/kind` package bundles plain go-tests (e.g. the helm-template contract tests like `TestHelmDeploymentTemplateRendersManagerPodAnnotations`) alongside the Ginkgo specs. One RED go-test fails the package and trips `make test-int` non-zero **even when both layers print `Ran X of Y … SUCCESS!`**. Always read the echoed `MAKE_EXIT` AND `grep -nE '^--- FAIL|^FAIL\s'` the log — not just the Ginkgo summary. (Phase 7 nearly tagged v1.0 on "14/14 green" while `make test-int` was red on a dropped chart-template block.)
49	- **A subagent's "pre-existing / unrelated" dismissal of a failing test is a claim, not verification.** Before relaying it as safe, git-archaeology the regressing commit (`git log -S '<token>' -- <file>`) and confirm it does not fail the gate. (Two Phase-7 agents waved off the dropped `podAnnotations` render block as pre-existing; it was a real ship-blocker — the verifier caught it and a `make test-int` re-run proved it.)
50	
51	## Operating Notes (Phase 02.2 lessons)
52	
53	Rules learned from 10 BLOCKED iterations across Phase 02.2. Trust them.
54	
55	- **Don't predict "chain terminator."** Plans 02.2-08/09/10 each predicted the end; all surfaced new cascades. Frame work as iterations; don't anchor on terminations.
56	- **Pre-empt worktree cwd-drift before merging.** Executor agents occasionally write artifacts to main's working tree instead of the worktree's. Pre-merge check:
57	  ```
58	  UNTRACKED=$(git status --porcelain | grep '^??' | grep "<plan>-" || true)
59	  ```
60	  If non-empty, diff against the worktree branch's commits; remove duplicates before `git merge`. (Plan 02.2-09 merge failed without this; Plan 02.2-10 succeeded with it.)
61	- **`gsd-sdk query state.begin-phase` resets STATE.md body fields.** It re-derives `Current Position` and `Status` from current plan inventory. Edit body fields AFTER `state.begin-phase` runs, not before, or the edit gets clobbered.
62	- **Question implicit framings when cascade classes diverge.** If 3+ consecutive cascades are the same class (e.g. cascades 2/3 both flag-mismatches), trust tactical iteration. If consecutive cascades differ in class (e.g. cascade-4 budget → cascade-5 harness-bug → cascade-6 timeouts), surface "is the underlying assumption still right?" before authoring the next plan. The cascade-8 debug session forced this question 7 plans late.
63	- **Use `--wave N` filter when a closeout plan is gated on a BLOCKED artifact.** Plan 02.2-02's `checkpoint:human-verify` reads `gate_decision: APPROVED` from a verification artifact; `--auto` mode would false-approve against a BLOCKED gate. Filter dispatch with `--wave <new-gap-wave>` until any plan records APPROVED and the rewire fires.
64	- **Subagent prompts: focus, don't restate.** Agents have their own system prompts that cover anti-patterns and protocols. `<additional_context>` blocks should carry decision-only context (the specific gap, the user's choice, the corrected hypothesis) — not the full cascade history or the entire anti-pattern table. Token budget directly affects subagent quality.
65	- **Honor `workflow.auto_advance: true` literally** — fire the chain across phase boundaries. But ASK FIRST at fix-shape decision points (Option A vs B at scope-defining moments). The user memory note covers this; respect it.
66	- **Decorative banners are noise unless they signal a real workflow transition.** `━━━ GSD ► STAGE ━━━` belongs at PLANNING / VERIFYING / EXECUTING boundaries (per `references/ui-brand.md`), not on every status update. Routine progress can be one-line.
67	- **End-of-turn summary: one or two sentences.** What changed and what's next. Resist the urge to recap multi-section status when the conversation thread is fresh.
68	- **Use TaskCreate for multi-cycle workflows.** A plan-check-commit-execute loop repeated 5+ times in one session genuinely benefits from task tracking. The system reminders about TaskCreate are not boilerplate.
69	- **STATE.md "Current Position" body text drifts.** It's prose, not derived; updates to it need to happen explicitly. The frontmatter (`progress.completed_plans`) is computed by SDK verbs; the body text is not.
70	- **Constrained-VM full-suite recipe (the dev Docker VM is ~7.65 GiB).** It DOES fit a clean `make test-int` if you don't accumulate state: delete → recreate → pre-warm (provisioner Ready + `kind load busybox:1.36`) a fresh kind cluster per heavy run, one heavy run at a time. Never let `make acceptance-v1-smoke` (it spins its OWN `tide-acceptance-<ts>` cluster) run while a `tide-test` cluster is still up — two single-node clusters OOM the node (exit 137). Phase 7's "env-gated, needs a bigger VM" deferral was actually solvable this way on the same 7.65 GiB host.
71	- **The acceptance `$0` path is a different deploy surface than Layer B — peel it one layer at a time.** Layer B fixtures carry a dummy `providerSecretRef` and use the test harness's helm install; the `$0` small-project has none and uses the chart defaults. So `$0` acceptance surfaces bugs Layer B masks (cascade-12 chart `default "latest"` image tags; cascade-13 credproxy hard-requiring `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`). Expect each green layer to expose the next; don't predict the terminator.
72	
73	## What this repository is
74	
75	**TIDE** (Topologically-Indexed Dependency Execution) — a Kubernetes-native orchestrator for hierarchical agentic coding work, designed to be open-sourced and to run against any project/codebase in any cluster. `README.md` is the design spec (and doubles as the public-facing README); check `git log` and `.planning/` for current implementation state.
76	
77	## The spec is load-bearing
78	
79	Everything downstream — schemas, APIs, controller logic, persistence — should trace back to the paradigm doc. When designing or implementing, preserve these distinctions; they exist for reasons the doc argues for explicitly:
80	
81	- **Five-level hierarchy**: Milestone → Phase → Plan → Task → Wave. Each level has its own artifact (`MILESTONE.md`, phase brief, `PLAN.md`, diff, execution schedule) and dependency model. Don't collapse levels or invent new ones; if a real implementation pressure pushes back on the hierarchy, update the spec first.
82	- **Two distinct DAGs**: the *Planning DAG* (which artifacts must exist before another can be authored — shallow, fans out wide) and the *Execution DAG* (which code must exist before another can be written — deeper, fans out narrow). Same Kahn-layered algorithm runs on both. APIs and CRDs should keep these typed apart, not unified into one "DAG" abstraction.
83	- **Waves are derived, not declared**. They are the output of layered Kahn on the task DAG. The orchestrator never accepts a wave list as input — only a DAG. Re-deriving on every plan edit is intentional (the spec calls this out: O(V+E), cheap, no stale-schedule caching).
84	- **Cycles are bugs, not runtime conditions**. Cycle detection happens at plan-validation time and a cyclic DAG refuses to run. Don't add "cycle recovery" features; reject and surface.
85	- **Failure semantics at wave boundaries** (spec §"Failure handling at wave boundaries") are specific: failed task → siblings in same wave continue (they were declared independent), dependents in later waves never dispatch, non-dependents in later waves dispatch in strict-by-default but halt in conservative profile. Keep this contract intact when implementing the executor.
86	- **Resumption state is minimal**: indegree map + completed-task set. If the persistence layer starts wanting to store the full schedule, that's a smell — re-derive instead.
87	
88	## Vocabulary conventions
89	
90	The water/tide metaphor is intentional and consistent — use it in code names, CRD names, log lines, and docs:
91	
92	- Rising tide = planning wave fanning out across subagents
93	- Slack tide = review checkpoint between waves
94	- Tidal lock = phase whose dependencies have all resolved
95	- Tidepool = sub-DAG developed in parallel isolation
96	- TIDE pod = deployment unit running a TIDE orchestrator (the K8s pun is intentional and load-bearing)
97	
98	Prefer extending the metaphor naturally over coining unrelated terms. If a name doesn't fit, prefer plain prose.

- **`git reset --hard`** outside a worktree's own HEAD-correction step.

- **Constrained-VM full-suite recipe (the dev Docker VM is ~7.65 GiB).** It DOES fit a clean `make test-int` if you don't accumulate state: delete → recreate → pre-warm (provisioner Ready + `kind load busybox:1.36`) a fresh kind cluster per heavy run, one heavy run at a time. ## What this repository is

**TIDE** (Topologically-Indexed Dependency Execution) — a Kubernetes-native orchestrator for hierarchical agentic coding work, designed to be open-sourced and to run against any project/codebase in any cluster. `README.md` is the design spec (and doubles as the public-facing README); check `git log` and `.planning/` for current implementation state.

## The spec is load-bearing

Everything downstream — schemas, APIs, controller logic, persistence — should trace back to the paradigm doc. When designing or implementing, preserve these distinctions; they exist for reasons the doc argues for explicitly:

- **Five-level hierarchy**: Milestone → Phase → Plan → Task → Wave. Each level has its own artifact (`MILESTONE.md`, phase brief, `PLAN.md`, diff, execution schedule) and dependency model. 100	## Implementation guidance
101	
102	- **Size planner and executor pools separately.** The spec argues planning fans out wide and execution fans out narrow — don't unify them into one worker pool.
103	- **Keep human-gate policy out of the controller.** Approve-every-milestone-but-auto-pass-plans must be as expressible as fully-autonomous or fully-supervised; the controller reads gate config, doesn't bake it in.
104	
105	(Provider/host/auth abstraction, pluggable subagent runtime, artifacts-as-source-of-truth, and resumability rules live in the Project Constraints and Stack Anti-patterns sections — don't restate here.)
106	
107	## Structural conventions in the spec document
108	
109	When editing `README.md` (the spec) itself:
110	
111	- Mermaid diagrams: nested `subgraph` containment for the planning graph; flat wave subgraphs with cross-wave edges for the execution graph. Match the existing style.
112	- Pseudocode uses Python-ish syntax with numbered-step comments (`# 1.`, `# 2.`).
113	- Worked examples follow pseudocode; the Kahn example walks the indegree map iteration-by-iteration. New algorithms get the same treatment.
114	- "Alternatives considered and rejected" is part of the doc's argumentative shape — when proposing a design choice, include the rejected alternatives, not just the winner.
115	- Voice is tight, declarative, em-dash-heavy. Match it rather than reverting to hedged corporate prose.
116	
117	<!-- GSD:project-start source:PROJECT.md -->
118	## Project
119	
120	**TIDE — Topologically-Indexed Dependency Execution**
121	
122	A Kubernetes-native orchestrator that runs hierarchical agentic coding work as a topologically-sorted DAG of subagent dispatches. A human applies a `Project` CRD (outcome prompt + target repo + creds); TIDE authors `MILESTONE.md`, phase briefs, `PLAN.md` files, and task diffs by dispatching specialist subagents at each level, parallelizing across waves derived from the declared task DAG. Built to be open-sourced and portable across clusters from day one.
123	
124	**Core Value:** **The five-level paradigm (Milestone → Phase → Plan → Task → Wave) runs as a real K8s orchestrator that can drive its own next milestone end-to-end.** If everything else fails, TIDE-on-TIDE must work — that's what proves the paradigm and the implementation simultaneously, and it's the bar for "v1 ships."
125	
126	### Constraints
127	
128	- **Tech stack**: Go + sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime + kubebuilder — K8s ecosystem default, idiomatic for CRDs/controllers, best contributor pool.
129	- **Tech stack**: Pluggable subagent runtime via a documented container image contract — never hard-coded to Anthropic SDK; v1 ships with a Claude-backed concrete impl behind the interface.
130	- **Distribution**: Apache 2.0, Helm chart from v1, designed for installation in arbitrary clusters with no hidden host dependencies.
131	- **Portability**: No hard-coded git host (GitHub, GitLab, Gitea must all work behind a generic git remote), no hard-coded LLM provider, no hard-coded auth model — abstract behind interfaces.
132	- **Persistence**: CRD `.status` only for v1 — no external DB, no SQLite. Per-object size stays well under etcd's 1.5 MiB hard limit by keeping per-Task CRDs small and label-indexed.
133	- **Failure semantics**: Wave boundary contract from spec §"Failure handling at wave boundaries" must be preserved exactly — failed task → siblings continue, dependents in later waves never dispatch, non-dependents dispatch in strict profile. Resumption state = indegree map + completed-task set, nothing more.
134	- **Resumability**: Long-running agentic work outlives single context windows. Every level boundary is a saved artifact; a fresh orchestrator restart re-derives waves from the task DAG + completed-task set in O(V+E).
135	- **Observability**: OpenTelemetry tracing must use OpenInference conventions for LLM/agent spans so traces are queryable in standard AI observability tools (Phoenix, LangSmith, Arize) without bespoke instrumentation.
136	<!-- GSD:project-end -->
137	
138	<!-- GSD:stack-start source:research/STACK.md -->
139	## Technology Stack
140	
141	Pinned versions. See `research/STACK.md` for rationale, alternatives, and citations.
142	
143	**Core:** Go 1.26 · controller-runtime v0.24.x · kubebuilder v4.14.0 · Kubernetes ≥ 1.33 (CEL CRD validation needs 1.29+) · Anthropic Go SDK v1.42.x · Claude Code CLI ≥ v2.1.139 · Helm 3 · Kustomize (kubebuilder-bundled).

## Implementation guidance

- **Size planner and executor pools separately.** The spec argues planning fans out wide and execution fans out narrow — don't unify them into one worker pool.
- **Keep human-gate policy out of the controller.** Approve-every-milestone-but-auto-pass-plans must be as expressible as fully-autonomous or fully-supervised; the controller reads gate config, doesn't bake it in.

(Provider/host/auth abstraction, pluggable subagent runtime, artifacts-as-source-of-truth, and resumability rules live in the Project Constraints and Stack Anti-patterns sections — don't restate here.)

## Structural conventions in the spec document

When editing `README.md` (the spec) itself:

- Mermaid diagrams: nested `subgraph` containment for the planning graph; flat wave subgraphs with cross-wave edges for the execution graph. Match the existing style.
- Pseudocode uses Python-ish syntax with numbered-step comments (`# 1.`, `# 2.`).
- Worked examples follow pseudocode; the Kahn example walks the indegree map iteration-by-iteration. New algorithms get the same treatment.
- "Alternatives considered and rejected" is part of the doc's argumentative shape — when proposing a design choice, include the rejected alternatives, not just the winner.
- Voice is tight, declarative, em-dash-heavy. Match it rather than reverting to hedged corporate prose.

<!-- GSD:project-start source:PROJECT.md -->
## Project

**TIDE — Topologically-Indexed Dependency Execution**

A Kubernetes-native orchestrator that runs hierarchical agentic coding work as a topologically-sorted DAG of subagent dispatches. A human applies a `Project` CRD (outcome prompt + target repo + creds); TIDE authors `MILESTONE.md`, phase briefs, `PLAN.md` files, and task diffs by dispatching specialist subagents at each level, parallelizing across waves derived from the declared task DAG. Built to be open-sourced and portable across clusters from day one.

**Core Value:** **The five-level paradigm (Milestone → Phase → Plan → Task → Wave) runs as a real K8s orchestrator that can drive its own next milestone end-to-end.** If everything else fails, TIDE-on-TIDE must work — that's what proves the paradigm and the implementation simultaneously, and it's the bar for "v1 ships."

### Constraints

- **Tech stack**: Go + sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime + kubebuilder — K8s ecosystem default, idiomatic for CRDs/controllers, best contributor pool.
- **Tech stack**: Pluggable subagent runtime via a documented container image contract — never hard-coded to Anthropic SDK; v1 ships with a Claude-backed concrete impl behind the interface.
- **Distribution**: Apache 2.0, Helm chart from v1, designed for installation in arbitrary clusters with no hidden host dependencies.
- **Portability**: No hard-coded git host (GitHub, GitLab, Gitea must all work behind a generic git remote), no hard-coded LLM provider, no hard-coded auth model — abstract behind interfaces.
- **Persistence**: CRD `.status` only for v1 — no external DB, no SQLite. Per-object size stays well under etcd's 1.5 MiB hard limit by keeping per-Task CRDs small and label-indexed.
- **Failure semantics**: Wave boundary contract from spec §"Failure handling at wave boundaries" must be preserved exactly — failed task → siblings continue, dependents in later waves never dispatch, non-dependents dispatch in strict profile. Resumption state = indegree map + completed-task set, nothing more.
- **Resumability**: Long-running agentic work outlives single context windows. Every level boundary is a saved artifact; a fresh orchestrator restart re-derives waves from the task DAG + completed-task set in O(V+E).
- **Observability**: OpenTelemetry tracing must use OpenInference conventions for LLM/agent spans so traces are queryable in standard AI observability tools (Phoenix, LangSmith, Arize) without bespoke instrumentation.
<!-- GSD:project-end -->

<!-- GSD:stack-start source:research/STACK.md -->
## Technology Stack

Pinned versions. See `research/STACK.md` for rationale, alternatives, and citations.

**Core:** Go 1.26 · controller-runtime v0.24.x · kubebuilder v4.14.0 · Kubernetes ≥ 1.33 (CEL CRD validation needs 1.29+) · Anthropic Go SDK v1.42.x · Claude Code CLI ≥ v2.1.139 · Helm 3 · Kustomize (kubebuilder-bundled).

**Supporting:** Ginkgo v2.28 + Gomega · envtest · kind v0.31 · logr v1.4 + zap v1.28 · prometheus/client_golang v1.23 · otel v1.43 (trace stable; metrics still v0.65 — keep metrics on client_golang) · OpenInference attribute names emitted on OTel spans (no Go SDK) · go-chi v5 · go-git v5.

**Dashboard:** React 18 + TypeScript · @xyflow/react v12 + dagre · Tailwind v4 · SSE (not WebSockets).

**Dev tools:** controller-gen · setup-envtest · golangci-lint (gosec, errcheck, staticcheck on) · helmify (Kustomize → Helm at release).

**Version coupling rules:**

- Never bump `k8s.io/*` independently — let controller-runtime's `go.mod` dictate.
- Pin Anthropic SDK to a minor (`v1.42.x`); it rev-bumps weekly with beta surfaces.
- OTel trace API is v1.x stable; metric/log APIs are v0.x — don't conflate the trains.
- Pin kind node images by `@sha256` in E2E scripts.

### Non-obvious choices to preserve

- **Layered Kahn in stdlib**, not Gonum or dominikbraun/graph. The spec's exposition is iteration-by-iteration; a graph library obscures it.
- **Native K8s Jobs**, not Argo or Tekton. The orchestrator owns the DAG; waves are derived, not declared as Workflow templates.
- **CRD `.status` only** — no external DB, no SQLite. Per-Task CRD stays small; the indegree map is in-process and rederivable.
- **OpenInference attribute names on OTel spans**, not the OTel GenAI semconv (still pre-stable in 2026). Phoenix/LangSmith/Arize consume OpenInference today.
- **CEL CRD validation (`x-kubernetes-validations`)**, not admission webhooks — except for cycle detection if CEL can't express all-paths.
- **zap behind logr**, not slog (~3× hot-path win for field-heavy reconcile logs).
- **chi router** as `manager.Runnable`, not gin/echo/fiber (fasthttp won't compose with controller-runtime's manager).
- **React Flow + DOM nodes**, not Cytoscape canvas. DOM lets every node be a real React component with live status.

### Anti-patterns

- Don't mount host `~/.claude/` into executor containers. Use `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env from a K8s Secret.
- Don't use Claude Code's OAuth flow headless (broken: claude-code#29983, #7100).
- Don't accept a wave list as CRD input — only a DAG. Don't cache the schedule in `.status` — rederive from the completed-task set.
- Don't hard-code one LLM provider, one git host, or one auth model in the controller. All Anthropic-specific code lives behind the `Subagent` interface in `internal/subagent/anthropic/`.
- Don't replace layered Kahn with CPM/HEFT. If pools become heterogeneous, add a wave-internal sub-scheduler behind Kahn.
- Don't add cycle "recovery" features. Refuse a cyclic DAG with a clear error.
- Don't vendor GSD Markdown. Re-implement planner/executor prompts as compiled-in Go templates.
- Default the chart's `ServiceMonitor` to `prometheus.enabled=false` to avoid CRD-not-found on plain clusters.

# examples/projects/dogfood/02-codex-runtime-project.yaml
#
# Run 2 of 3 in the TIDE-on-TIDE dogfood sequence.
# Apply ONLY after 01-analytics-project reaches `status.phase: Complete`.
# Do NOT apply 03-project-editor-project.yaml until this Project reaches
# `status.phase: Complete` — the Project CRD has no ordering mechanism;
# sequencing is operator discipline.
#
# Purpose: add a second Subagent implementation (Codex / OpenAI) to TIDE and
# demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — actually running mixed-provider
# waves, not just proving the interface compiles.
#
# After this run completes: the run's token spend, dispatch behavior, and
# failure characteristics are visible on the analytics dashboard built by run 1.
# Run 3 (Project editor) can then be authored and applied through the dashboard
# surface run 3 itself will build.
#
# Prerequisites (see README.md for full recipe):
#   - 01-analytics-project must have reached status.phase: Complete
#   - kind cluster + TIDE charts installed (see docs/INSTALL.md)
#   - tide-secrets Secret created in namespace tide-dogfood-codex
#     (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + GIT_PAT — same as analytics namespace)
#   - openai-secrets Secret created in namespace tide-dogfood-codex
#     (OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY — see README.md)
#   - per-namespace-resources applied per README.md
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: tide-dogfood-codex
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: tide-sample
    tideproject.k8s/sample: dogfood-codex
---
apiVersion: tideproject.k8s/v1alpha1
kind: Project
metadata:
  name: dogfood-codex-runtime
  namespace: tide-dogfood-codex
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: tide-sample
    tideproject.k8s/sample: dogfood-codex
spec:
  # TIDE operates against its own repo — TIDE-on-TIDE dogfood run.
  targetRepo: https://github.com/jsquirrelz/tide.git

  outcomePrompt: |
    ## Outcome

    Add a second Subagent implementation for the Codex runtime (OpenAI) and
    demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — mixed-provider waves that actually
    run, not just interface scaffolding. Concrete outcome: a TIDE operator can
    configure a Project where the planner pool runs Claude and the executor pool
    runs Codex, and TIDE dispatches a multi-phase run to completion with correct
    wave-boundary failure semantics across both providers.

    ## Architecture — locked decisions (do NOT reinvent these)

    **Implementation location: internal/subagent/codex/**
    Mirror the existing internal/subagent/anthropic/ layering exactly:
    - internal/subagent/codex/client.go — Client struct + constructor
    - internal/subagent/codex/run.go — Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)
    - internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile — multi-stage build mirroring anthropic/Dockerfile
    - internal/subagent/codex/doc.go — package doc referencing D-C1 layering pattern

    All OpenAI/Codex-specific code stays behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent
    interface. No provider hard-coding in the controller. No Codex imports
    in internal/controller/ or cmd/manager/.

    **Per-level runtime selection (not per-task, not per-project).**
    Planner pool and executor pool each pin a runtime. This is the correct
    granularity: it matches the spec's separate-pools argument, achieves real
    heterogeneity within a single run, and avoids the API/scheduler complexity
    of per-task selection. Rejected alternatives (do not reopen):
    - Per-task selection: large API surface, non-trivial scheduler changes.
    - Per-project selection: no mixing within a run (defeats heterogeneity goal).

    The SubagentConfig field already has Levels.{Milestone,Phase,Plan,Task}
    per-level LevelConfig overrides (api/v1alpha1/project_types.go). Wire the
    Codex image at the levels the operator wants to run Codex.

    **Credential path.**
    OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY via a K8s Secret — the established
    Secret-ref pattern from providerSecretRef. Never mount host config, never
    headless OAuth (broken: claude-code#29983, #7100 — same restriction applies
    to Codex). Chart values for the new Secret name are part of the deliverable.

    **Mixed-provider waves: identical wave-boundary failure semantics.**
    The spec's failure handling (spec section "Failure handling at wave
    boundaries") applies unchanged regardless of which provider runs a task:
    - Failed task: siblings in the same wave continue (they were declared
      independent).
    - Dependents in later waves: never dispatch.
    - Non-dependents in later waves: dispatch in strict profile.
    This contract must not change when tasks run on different providers.

    **Codex CLI facts — carry verbatim, do NOT re-research:**
    - codex exec is first-class headless: one-shot, no TTY, final message
      to stdout.
    - Flags for clean containers: --ephemeral, --skip-git-repo-check,
      --ignore-user-config.
    - --json emits a JSONL event stream.
    - --output-schema <file> constrains the final response to a JSON schema
      — use this for child-CRD emission (child-CRD format is JSON, mandated).
    - Sandbox defaults read-only; the runner needs --sandbox workspace-write
      (fine inside an already-isolated pod — mirrors the Claude runner posture).
    - API-key env auth (OPENAI_API_KEY) is the documented container path —
      no headless-OAuth problem.
    - MCP client support (STDIO + streamable HTTP) via config.toml or
      codex mcp add — available if needed for child-CRD emission.
    - Sources: developers.openai.com/codex/{noninteractive,cli/reference,
      auth,cli/features} (verified 2026-06).

    **Container image.**
    internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile must build clean. The final image
    bundles the Codex CLI binary. Image reference convention mirrors
    ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:1.0.0 (replace "claude" with
    "codex" in the image name; exact tag/registry are authoring decisions).

    ## Constraints

    - All Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays in internal/subagent/codex/ and
      behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent interface — no provider leakage into
      the controller or cmd/manager.
    - Wave-boundary failure semantics unchanged for mixed-provider waves.
    - Secret material never inlined in manifests or chart values — reference
      by Secret name only.
    - Follow all CLAUDE.md conventions: Apache 2.0 headers, logr/zap logging,
      controller-runtime patterns.
    - Do NOT break existing tests. Confirm make test is green before
      authoring any plan as delivered.
    - Do NOT add a real OpenAI API call in tests without an explicit test-env
      guard (OPENAI_API_KEY env set) — tests must pass offline.

  # Phase 2 D-C1..C4: credproxy reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from this Secret.
  # For the planner pool (Claude side of the heterogeneous run).
  # The Codex executor pool's OPENAI_API_KEY lives in a separate Secret —
  # see README.md for the openai-secrets creation step.
  providerSecretRef: tide-secrets

  # Starting cap for dogfood runs — tune before applying.
  # These are multi-phase real work runs; the cap is a safety net.
  budget:
    absoluteCapCents: 10000
    rollingWindowCapCents: 10000
    rollingWindowDuration: 24h

  # Default image + model for the planner pool (Claude).
  # Codex executor pool is wired via subagent.levels.task.image override
  # once the codex subagent image is built by this run.
  # Cheaper alternatives: model: claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5
  subagent:
    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:1.0.0
    model: claude-opus-4-7

  # Phase 3 D-B6: per-project git target + creds.
  git:
    repoURL: https://github.com/jsquirrelz/tide.git
    credsSecretRef: tide-secrets

  # Human oversight at milestone and phase level; plan and task auto.
  # Fully-autonomous: change milestone and phase to auto.
  gates:
    milestone: approve
    phase: approve
    plan: auto
    task: auto
    pauseBetweenWaves: false

  planAdmission:
    fileTouchMode: strict

  maxAttemptsPerTask: 3

    headless OAuth (broken: claude-code#29983, #7100 — same restriction applies

    boundaries") applies unchanged regardless of which provider runs a task:
    - Failed task: siblings in the same wave continue (they were declared
      independent).
    - Dependents in later waves: never dispatch.
    - Non-dependents in later waves: dispatch in strict profile.
    This contract must not change when tasks run on different providers.

    **Codex CLI facts — carry verbatim, do NOT re-research:**
    - codex exec is first-class headless: one-shot, no TTY, final message
      to stdout.
    - Flags for clean containers: --ephemeral, --skip-git-repo-check,
      --ignore-user-config.
    - --json emits a JSONL event stream.
    - --output-schema <file> constrains the final response to a JSON schema
      — use this for child-CRD emission (child-CRD format is JSON, mandated).
    - Sandbox defaults read-only; the runner needs --sandbox workspace-write
      (fine inside an already-isolated pod — mirrors the Claude runner posture).
    - API-key env auth (OPENAI_API_KEY) is the documented container path —
      no headless-OAuth problem.
    - MCP client support (STDIO + streamable HTTP) via config.toml or

      controller-runtime patterns.

      authoring any plan as delivered.

  gates:

16	
17	package anthropic
18	
19	import (
20		"encoding/json"
21		"fmt"
22		"io"
23	
24		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/subagent/common"
25		pkgdispatch "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/pkg/dispatch"
26	)
27	
28	// streamEvent is the verified Claude Code stream-json line shape (RESEARCH
29	// Pattern 5 lines 502-523). Only the fields Phase 3 actually consumes are
30	// declared; everything else (subtype, session_id, durations, model_usage)
31	// rides through to events.jsonl untouched for Phase 4 OpenInference parsing.
32	//
33	// JSON tags use snake_case to match the CLI's top-level shape. The per-model
34	// nested model_usage uses camelCase per the CLI's TypeScript pass-through —
35	// Phase 3 ignores that nested map because Phase 2's Usage struct does not
36	// expose per-model breakdown yet.
37	type streamEvent struct {
38		Type   string       `json:"type"`
39		Result string       `json:"result,omitempty"`
40		Usage  *streamUsage `json:"usage,omitempty"`
41	}
42	
43	// streamUsage mirrors the top-level `usage` block in a stream-json result
44	// event. Field names use snake_case to match what the CLI emits; the parser
45	// maps them to camelCase pkg/dispatch.Usage fields per D-C5 (CacheReadTokens,
46	// CacheCreationTokens drop the "Input" infix because Phase 2's
47	// pkg/dispatch.Usage already separates input/output via dedicated fields).
48	type streamUsage struct {
49		InputTokens              int64 `json:"input_tokens"`
50		OutputTokens             int64 `json:"output_tokens"`
51		CacheReadInputTokens     int64 `json:"cache_read_input_tokens"`
52		CacheCreationInputTokens int64 `json:"cache_creation_input_tokens"`
53	}
54	
55	// ParseStream reads JSONL stream-json events from r, tees the raw bytes to
56	// rawSink (the per-Task events.jsonl audit log for Phase 4 OpenInference),
57	// and returns the final assistant text + token tally extracted from the
58	// terminal result event.
59	//
60	// Per D-C5: maps snake_case stream-json keys → camelCase pkg/dispatch.Usage
61	// fields:
62	//
63	//	input_tokens                → Usage.InputTokens
64	//	output_tokens               → Usage.OutputTokens
65	//	cache_read_input_tokens     → Usage.CacheReadTokens
66	//	cache_creation_input_tokens → Usage.CacheCreationTokens
67	//
68	// Defensive parsing: non-JSON lines (rare per RESEARCH Pattern 5) are written
69	// to rawSink and silently skipped — they do not cause ParseStream to return
70	// an error. The rationale (RESEARCH Pattern 5 line 555): "tolerate non-JSON
71	// lines (rare, but defensive)" — the audit log records everything; the
72	// orchestrator gets the structured tally.
73	//
74	// The 16MB per-line budget is enforced by [common.ReadLines]; lines longer
75	// than that return an error.
76	func ParseStream(r io.Reader, rawSink io.Writer) (pkgdispatch.Usage, string, error) {
77		var (
78			usage      pkgdispatch.Usage
79			resultText string
80		)
81	
82		err := common.ReadLines(r, func(line []byte) error {
83			// Tee raw bytes (with newline) to events.jsonl. Phase 4 OpenInference
84			// parsing reads this file untouched — Phase 3 only consumes the
85			// terminal result event.
86			if _, werr := rawSink.Write(append(line, '\n')); werr != nil {
87				return fmt.Errorf("anthropic: write events.jsonl: %w", werr)
88			}
89	
90			var ev streamEvent
91			if jerr := json.Unmarshal(line, &ev); jerr != nil {
92				// Tolerate non-JSON lines — they have already been teed to
93				// rawSink for Phase 4 forensic analysis.
94				return nil
95			}
96	
97			if ev.Type == "result" {
98				resultText = ev.Result
99				if ev.Usage != nil {
100					usage.InputTokens = ev.Usage.InputTokens
101					usage.OutputTokens = ev.Usage.OutputTokens
102					// D-C5 snake_case → camelCase mapping. Drop the "Input"
103					// infix on cache fields: pkg/dispatch.Usage separates input
104					// vs output via dedicated fields, so qualifying the cache
105					// counters with "Input" would be redundant.
106					usage.CacheReadTokens = ev.Usage.CacheReadInputTokens
107					usage.CacheCreationTokens = ev.Usage.CacheCreationInputTokens
108				}
109			}
110			return nil
111		})
112		if err != nil {
113			return usage, resultText, err
114		}
115		return usage, resultText, nil
116	}
117

# PLAN.md — plan-03-codex-usage-normalizer

## Scope

Add the `internal/subagent/codex/` package implementing `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` for
the OpenAI Codex runtime, wire the per-level vendor switch in the controller so operators
can configure heterogeneous mixed-provider waves (e.g. Claude planners + Codex executors),
produce the `cmd/codex-subagent` binary entry point and its container image, and extend
the Helm chart + dogfood example manifests to document the new capability.

## Acceptance criteria (whole plan)

1. `go build ./...` is clean — no compilation errors.
2. `make test` (unit tests) is green with no OPENAI_API_KEY required.
3. `internal/subagent/codex/` satisfies the `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` interface and rejects
   envelopes whose `Provider.Vendor != "openai"` at the vendor sentinel check.
4. `internal/subagent/codex/pricing.go` contains an OpenAI model price table; `estimatedCostCents`
   returns 0 for zero-token usage and a conservative-tier fallback on unknown models.
5. `internal/subagent/codex/stream_parser.go` maps `prompt_tokens` → `InputTokens`,
   `completion_tokens` → `OutputTokens`, `prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens` → `CacheReadTokens`,
   and keeps `CacheCreationTokens = 0`.
6. `internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go` `ResolveProvider` derives Vendor from
   `LevelConfig.Vendor` → `SubagentConfig.Vendor` → fallback `"anthropic"`, and all
   existing `ResolveProvider` tests pass unchanged.
7. `api/v1alpha1/project_types.go` `LevelConfig` carries a `Vendor string` field and
   `SubagentConfig` carries a `Vendor string` field; `zz_generated.deepcopy.go` is
   NOT modified (the `*out = *in` pattern handles plain string fields correctly).
8. `images/codex-subagent/Dockerfile` builds clean via multi-stage build and bundles
   the `codex` CLI binary from the official npm package.
9. `charts/tide/values.yaml` contains a `codexSubagent` image entry under `images:`;
   `examples/projects/dogfood/02-codex-runtime-project.yaml` demonstrates per-level
   vendor wiring (`subagent.levels.task.vendor: openai`).

---

## Tasks

### task-01-codex-subagent-pkg
**Objective:** Create the complete `internal/subagent/codex/` Go package — five source
files (doc.go, client.go, run.go, pricing.go, stream_parser.go) — implementing
`pkg/dispatch.Subagent` behind the provider firewall.

**Dependencies:** none (Wave 1)

**Files touched:**
- `internal/subagent/codex/doc.go`

- `internal/subagent/codex/run.go`
- `internal/subagent/codex/pricing.go`
- `internal/subagent/codex/stream_parser.go`

**Acceptance:** `go build ./internal/subagent/codex/` succeeds; vendor sentinel check
rejects non-"openai" envelopes; estimatedCostCents returns conservative fallback on
unknown models; ParseStream maps OpenAI usage fields to pkg/dispatch.Usage.

---

### task-02-codex-tests
**Objective:** Author offline-safe unit tests for the Codex subagent package:
`subagent_test.go` (vendor sentinel + fake exec), `pricing_test.go` (price table
coverage + conservative fallback), `stream_parser_test.go` (JSONL event parsing).
No real OPENAI_API_KEY is used — all tests must pass offline.

**Dependencies:** task-01-codex-subagent-pkg (Wave 2)

**Files touched:**
- `internal/subagent/codex/subagent_test.go`
- `internal/subagent/codex/pricing_test.go`
- `internal/subagent/codex/stream_parser_test.go`

**Acceptance:** `go test ./internal/subagent/codex/...` passes with no network calls.

---

### task-03-codex-cmd
**Objective:** Create `cmd/codex-subagent/main.go` — the thin binary entry point that
mirrors `cmd/claude-subagent/main.go`: reads `EnvelopeIn` → calls `codex.Run()` →
writes `EnvelopeOut`. Wires `TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON`, worktree setup, and commit.

**Dependencies:** task-01-codex-subagent-pkg (Wave 2)

**Files touched:**
- `cmd/codex-subagent/main.go`

**Acceptance:** `go build ./cmd/codex-subagent/` produces a binary; structure mirrors

---

compiles the `cmd/codex-subagent` Go binary statically and bundles it with the Codex CLI
npm package (`@openai/codex`) in a node:22-slim base image, mirroring
